name: Cheese Fondue #1
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the cheese in a plastic bag and sprinkle with flour. Toss until the cheese is evenly coated.
2. Rub the cut clove of garlic on the bottom and sides of a 3-quart saucepan.
3. Add wine to the saucepan and heat over low heat just until bubbles rise to the surface (make sure the wine does not boil).
4. Stir in lemon juice, then add cheese, about half a cup at a time, stirring constantly with a wooden spoon.
5. Continue adding cheese until it is fully melted. Stir in Kirsch.
6. Pour the mixture into a ceramic fondue pot over low heat.
7. Use long-handled forks to spear bread cubes and dip them into the fondue using a stirring motion.
NOTE: The Swiss cheese should be aged for at least 6 months.
